title: Office of Profit
lead: The concept lies at the confluence of ==constitutional morality, political ethics and separation of powers== — yet the law ==does not define== what constitutes an office of profit; courts have built it case by case.

items: ==Separation of power== between legislature and executive; preventing a ==conflict between duty and interest== in a person who is both legislator and executive.
==2018==: on ECI recommendation, the President disqualified ==20 Delhi MLAs== for holding Parliamentary Secretary posts.
Parliamentary secretaries appointed in ==~20 states== — courts struck down several such appointments.

head: Element
Case
Principle
rows: (1) holds an office, (2) it is an office ==of profit==, (3) it is ==under the Government==.
Extent of ==government control== over the office is pivotal.
==Profit is necessary== — mere prestige is insufficient.
Holder must be ==legally capable== of holding the office.

items.title: The Four Tests — Swapan Roy v. Pradyut Bordoloi (2001)
bullets: ==Government appointment==: power to appoint and remove ([[Guru Govinda Basu (1964)]])

items.title: Second ARC Recommendations
bullets: Purely ==advisory bodies== ≠ office of profit, regardless of remuneration
==Executive decision-making power + control over public funds== = office of profit
Offices requiring ==close CoM coordination== essential to government functioning should NOT be treated as offices of profit

blocks.text: A practical view, not a pedantic basket of tests, should guide in arriving at a sensible conclusion.
blocks.by: Prof. P.D.T. Achary, Practice and Procedure of Parliament
